# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create Kubernetes deployment manifests for microservices. Add annotations to control Istio sidecar injection behavior. Set sidecar.istio.io/inject to manage proxy injection per deployment. This is for our service mesh deployment strategy.
# Category: istio_sidecar_injection_disabled

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: microservice-name # replace with your unique identifier here (e.g., myapp)
spec:  
  selector:
    matchLabels:    
      app : microservice-label # replace 'microservices' and change it to the label that you use for identifying each deployment in Kubernetes e.g, applicationName or name of your service/application running on cluster (e.g., myapp) 
       -->  matchLabels: {App="my_service"} # update as per requirement here with correct microservices and their corresponding labels like "micro-customer", etc..  
          
---   
apiVersion: v1               
kind: Service             
metadata:                
  name : myapp           
spec:                    
  selector:              
     app: mysql             # replace 'mysql' with the service you want to expose on your cluster, e.g., db for a MySQL database instance in this case    --> selects Pod(Label Selector) where microservice label is same as name of that pod      "myapp"  and use appropriate labels
     tier: mysql             # replace 'tier' with the specific service you want to expose, e.g., web for a Web Server in this case    --> selects Pod(Label Selector) where microservice label is same as name of that pod      "web"  and use appropriate labels
     port :                 # Replace PORT number (3017/5432 etc.) with the actual exposed ports on your Kubernetes cluster.   --> Use correct values corresponding to service, which should expose its applications in this case using 'PORT' numbers for each microservice as per requirement
     
--- 
apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1alpha3             #Istio sidecar injection              --->  use appropriate version of ISTIO API here   (i.e., v1 alpha-x)        --> Use latest available until a stable release is made     "--> selects Pod(Label Selector)"
kind: Gateway                      
metadata :                          #replace with your unique identifier    gateway  e.g myapp_gateway            --->  use appropriate values here       microservice, app and name   --> Use correct label or names to identify the services in this case      "microservices", applicationName="myapplication". Gateways are not associated directly with individual pods but have sidecars
    annotations:                  #annotate Istio for gateway injection.              --->  use appropriate values here     app, name and microservice  --> Use correct labels or names to identify the services in this case      "applicationName="my_app". This will be used as prefix when creating virtual hosts."
        sidecar-injector: mix                #the annotations should reflect exactly how you want your Istio SideCar Injection Annotation. For example, if not specified explicitly the operator would default to true/false depending on whether Kubernetes network policies are enabled (for now). The 'mix' value is just used as a placeholder here for future use cases
    name : myapp-gateway            #replace with your unique identifier       -->  Use appropriate values and names, e.g., appname or serviceName   "my_application" —> selects Pod(Label Selector) where microservice label is same as 'microservices' which match the corresponding services in this case
spec :                          #Replace PORT number (3017/5432 etc.) with your actual exposed ports on Kubernetes cluster.      -->  use appropriate values here e.g., 80,90   or any port from user specified value such as app-port and expose it by creating corresponding label in service specification which is used for selecting microservice pods
    selector :                     #Selecting services that are defined inside Gateway configuration (e.g mysql). It should match the name of each Microservices you want to proxy requests, e.g., db   -->  use appropriate values here and 'my_app' as prefix if needed  "db" or with a suitable label/name for your microservice
      app : my-mysql                 #replace it on both parts: service (kind of the pod) name ('microservices') must match exactly in selector part   -->  use appropriate values here, e.g., 'my_app', and specific labels as per requirement "db" or with a suitable label/name for your microservice
      tier : mysql                   #replace it on both parts: service (kind of the pod) name ('microservices') must match exactly in selector part   -->  use appropriate values here, e.g., 'web' and specific labels as per requirement "db" or with a suitable label/name for your microservice
     ports :                        #Replace PORT number(3017 /5432 etc.)  to the actual exposed port on Kubernetes cluster        -->  use appropriate values here e.g.,80,90    , and expose it by creating corresponding labels in service specification which is used for selecting microservice pods
       name : http                   #replace with your unique identifier   ----> Use correct names like 'app', or suitable ones based on requirement  "web" -->  use appropriate values here e.g., app-port, and expose it by creating corresponding label in service specification which is used for selecting microservice pods
         port :                      #Replace PORT number to the actual exposed ports On Kubernetes cluster        ----> Use correct value based on requirement 3017 or other as per your need.   "80" -->  use appropriate values here e.g., '90'    , and expose it by creating corresponding label in service specification which is used for selecting microservice pods
